acceptance_targets: [ch15-craft-001] PASS if the revised Farah passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:137 and the revised Gabe passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:341 contain at least three concrete spoken specifics that are currently summarized, and at least one listener reaction is triggered by a stated detail rather than generic bad-news recognition. FAIL if either scene still reports that Lena 'told' them without rendering the humiliating content itself.

[ch15-dialogue-001] PASS if at least two of Farah's revised lines in the cited spans deliver care through concrete instruction, practical question, or rough local shorthand rather than abstract reassurance or extended metaphor, and she still reads as dry and unsentimental. FAIL if she still sounds like the chapter's most quotable counselor instead of a specific woman from this block.

[ch15-prose-001] PASS if the revised archive section includes one audible Eli fragment rendered on page with a bodily consequence for Lena, and no more than one of the cited midchapter transitions still uses a 'Lena looked ...' construction. FAIL if Eli remains entirely unheard in the archive scene or if the chapter still leans on repeated visual glance beats to pivot emotional turns.

[HIGH_CH15_ARCHIVE_LEDGER_WRONG_HISTORY] Pass if adding the line items at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6736-6752 yields the stated total and every listed payment corresponds to a money event that exists elsewhere in the revised novel. Fail if the total only works by introducing ghost transactions or revised amounts that the reader has never actually seen.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised Farah passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:137 and the revised Gabe passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:341 contain at least three concrete spoken specifics that are currently summarized, and at least one listener reaction is triggered by a stated detail rather than generic bad-news recognition. FAIL if either scene still reports that Lena 'told' them without rendering the humiliating content itself.
findings.evidence: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:137, Lena's first account to Farah collapses into reported summary ('So Lena told her ... But she gave enough') instead of making her voice the ugliest facts. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:349, the Gabe disclosure does the same ('She told him about the photos ... about proof on a laptop'). The chapter's engine is the shame of telling other people what happened, but the telling itself is repeatedly paraphrased.
findings.problem: Sanitization by summary: the chapter repeatedly steps around Lena having to say the most humiliating specifics aloud, so private humiliation becomes social fact more cleanly than this premise can bear.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:137, replace the summary block with on-page speech that makes Lena name June's authorship, the templated money asks, and at least one sexual-control detail from the archive. In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:341 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:349, keep Gabe ignorant of June's name if that remains the chapter design, but make Lena say at least two concrete humiliations beyond 'fake profile and software' such as stolen source media, coached sexual direction, or scripted money requests.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of Farah's revised lines in the cited spans deliver care through concrete instruction, practical question, or rough local shorthand rather than abstract reassurance or extended metaphor, and she still reads as dry and unsentimental. FAIL if she still sounds like the chapter's most quotable counselor instead of a specific woman from this block.
findings.evidence: Farah's strongest lines are vivid and in character, but several later lines turn polished enough to blur her dry logistics-first idiolect with the narrator's house voice: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:211 ('Money is countable, so it behaves better. The rest doesn't.'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:221 ('amputation in places you didn't know had nerves'), and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:261 ('you need a wall, not a crowbar').
findings.problem: Farah intermittently drifts from grounded neighborhood pragmatist into aphoristic therapist-sage language, which weakens idiolect separation and pushes against the style bible's instruction that she offers care through logistics rather than speeches.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen and localize Farah's support lines around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:211, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:221, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:261. Keep her dry humor and competence, but route at least two of those beats through bottle-shop language, task assignment, interruption, or clipped anger instead of finished metaphor or therapy framing.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised archive section includes one audible Eli fragment rendered on page with a bodily consequence for Lena, and no more than one of the cited midchapter transitions still uses a 'Lena looked ...' construction. FAIL if Eli remains entirely unheard in the archive scene or if the chapter still leans on repeated visual glance beats to pivot emotional turns.
findings.evidence: The archive scene foregrounds voice-note evidence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:13 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:57, but Lena never actually hears one; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:15 explicitly retreats to transcript. After that, several emotional turns are staged through repeated look-beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:209, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:223, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:445.
findings.problem: The prose pulls back from the book's sound-driven premise at the exact moment the archive should be most destabilizing, then defaults too often to neutral visual staging. That combination reads as timidity plus a small recurring formula, and it flattens a chapter that should feel acoustically haunted.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:13 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:16 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:55 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:57, make Lena play or vividly hear one Eli artifact so the scene uses the style bible's sound bias instead of only remembered transcript. Around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:209, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:223, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:445, replace most 'Lena looked ...' transitions with touch, breath, posture, or spatial-pressure beats.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if adding the line items at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6736-6752 yields the stated total and every listed payment corresponds to a money event that exists elsewhere in the revised novel. Fail if the total only works by introducing ghost transactions or revised amounts that the reader has never actually seen.
findings.problem: Lena's forensic ledger invents a different financial history: $380, $460, $575, and $2,750 totaling $4,165. Those numbers do not match the dramatized transfers or the reveal log. This chapter is where the romance becomes evidence and social fact; if the archive math is wrong here, the book's documentary authority collapses exactly where it should harden.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6736-6752, rewrite the itemized archive list so the dates, purposes, and amounts mirror the canonical transfer spine. If the novel truly needs an additional off-page transfer beyond the scenes already dramatized, this chapter must identify it cleanly as a distinct payment with a clear purpose instead of silently replacing existing figures; otherwise restrict the ledger to the established transfers and recalculate the total.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
